# terrible_community_general_newrelic_deployment (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against
- `revision` (String) A revision number (for example, git commit SHA).
- `token` (String) API token to place in the Api-Key header.

### Optional

- `app_name` (String) The value of `app_name` in the `newrelic.yml` file used by the application. One of `app_name` or `application_id` is required.
- `app_name_exact_match` (Boolean) If this flag is set to `true` then the application ID lookup by name would only work for an exact match. If set to `false` it returns the first result.
- `application_id` (String) The application ID found in the metadata of the application in APM. One of `app_name` or `application_id` is required.
- `changelog` (String) A list of changes for this deployment.
- `description` (String) Text annotation for the deployment - notes for you.
- `user` (String) The name of the user/process that triggered this deployment.
- `validate_certs` (Boolean) If `false`, SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.
